global_config.visual_style: Modern, realistic, slice-of-life anime style, vibrant colors, cinematic lighting.
global_config.setting_summary_cn: 一个关于大学生对电脑和网络的依赖，以及因此引发的朋友间小矛盾的故事。主要场景发生在学生宿舍和KTV门口。
global_config.setting_summary_en: A story about a college student's reliance on computers and the internet, leading to a minor conflict with friends. Main settings are a student dorm room and the entrance of a KTV.

character_definitions.name: 张天明
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A male Chinese college student, around 20. Wears glasses and casual clothes like a hoodie. Looks a bit nerdy and often engrossed in his thoughts or his screen.

character_definitions.name: 丽莎
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A female Chinese college student, around 20. Stylish and expressive, with fashionable clothes. She is not afraid to show her emotions, especially impatience and anger.

character_definitions.name: 柯林
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A male Chinese college student, around 20. Looks friendly, easy-going, and tech-savvy. Wears modern, comfortable street wear.

character_definitions.name: 雪梅
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A female Chinese college student, around 20. Has a studious and gentle appearance. Dresses more conservatively than Lisha.

hanzi: 张天明是个电脑迷,他的电脑从早到晚都开着。他在网上看新闻、查资料、玩儿游戏,有时候还在自己的网站上写博客,一会儿都离不开电脑。他常常一上网,就忘了时间。
pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng shì ge diànnǎo mí tā de diànnǎo cóng zǎo dào wǎn dōu kāi zhe tā zài wǎng shàng kàn xīnwén chá zīliào wánr yóuxì yǒu shíhou hái zài zìjǐ de wǎngzhàn shàng xiě bókè yīhuìr dōu lí bu kāi diànnǎo tā chángcháng yī shàng wǎng jiù wàng le shíjiān
translation: Zhang Tianming is a computer fanatic; his computer is on from morning till night. He reads news, looks for information, and plays games online. Sometimes he also writes on his blog. He can't tear himself away from his computer for a moment. As soon as he goes online, he loses track of time.

scenes.scene_goal: 介绍张天明这个角色，并展示他对电脑的极度沉迷。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 【蒙太奇】快速切换张天明在宿舍电脑前的各种活动。窗外光线从白天变为黑夜，但他始终专注于屏幕，时而严肃，时而发笑，完全忘记了时间。
scenes.setting_en: A college student's dorm room, dominated by a glowing computer setup.
scenes.shot_type: Medium shot, close-up on face and screen.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Fast-paced montage of a young Asian male student, Zhang Tianming, in his dorm room, completely absorbed by his computer from morning till night. Quick cuts show him browsing news, playing video games with intense focus, and typing on his blog. The light from the window changes from bright sun to dark night, showing the passage of time. He is a true computer fanatic.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 张天明是个电脑迷,他的电脑从早到晚都开着。他在网上看新闻、查资料、玩儿游戏,有时候还在自己的网站上写博客,一会儿都离不开电脑。他常常一上网,就忘了时间。
voice_over.pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng shì ge diànnǎo mí tā de diànnǎo cóng zǎo dào wǎn dōu kāi zhe tā zài wǎng shàng kàn xīnwén chá zīliào wánr yóuxì yǒu shíhou hái zài zìjǐ de wǎngzhàn shàng xiě bókè yīhuìr dōu lí bu kāi diànnǎo tā chángcháng yī shàng wǎng jiù wàng le shíjiān
voice_over.translation: Zhang Tianming is a computer fanatic; his computer is on from morning till night. He reads news, looks for information, and plays games online. Sometimes he also writes on his blog. He can't tear himself away from his computer for a moment. As soon as he goes online, he loses track of time.

hanzi: 昨天,丽莎、柯林、雪梅和他约好今天一起去唱卡拉OK,时间到了他也没来,打他的手机,也不接,就给他发了一个电子邮件。
pinyin: zuótiān Lìshā Kē Lín Xuěméi hé tā yuē hǎo jīntiān yìqǐ qù chàng kǎlā'ōukēi shíjiān dào le tā yě méi lái dǎ tā de shǒujī yě bù jiē jiù gěi tā fā le yí ge diànzǐ yóujiàn
translation: Yesterday, Lisha, Ke Lin, Xuemei, and he had arranged to go to karaoke today. When the time came, he didn't show up. They called his cell phone, but he didn't answer, so they sent him an email.

scenes.scene_goal: 展示朋友们在等待迟到的张天明，并表现出丽莎的不满。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 夜晚，KTV门口霓虹闪烁。丽莎、柯林、雪梅在焦急等待。丽莎不耐烦地看手机，打电话无人接听后，她生气地在手机上快速打字发信息。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot, with a rack focus to Lisha's phone.
scenes.shot_type: Medium shot, close-up on Lisha's face and phone.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Outside a flashy KTV entrance with neon lights at night. Three friends, Lisha, Ke Lin, and Xuemei, are waiting. Lisha looks at her phone impatiently, tries to make a call, and gets frustrated when there's no answer. She then angrily types a message on her phone.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 昨天,丽莎、柯林、雪梅和他约好今天一起去唱卡拉OK,时间到了他也没来,打他的手机,也不接,就给他发了一个电子邮件。
voice_over.pinyin: zuótiān Lìshā Kē Lín Xuěméi hé tā yuē hǎo jīntiān yìqǐ qù chàng kǎlā'ōukēi shíjiān dào le tā yě méi lái dǎ tā de shǒujī yě bù jiē jiù gěi tā fā le yí ge diànzǐ yóujiàn
voice_over.translation: Yesterday, Lisha, Ke Lin, Xuemei, and he had arranged to go to karaoke today. When the time came, he didn't show up. They called his cell phone, but he didn't answer, so they sent him an email.

hanzi: 十分钟以后,才看见天明急急忙忙地跑过来。
pinyin: shí fēnzhōng yǐhòu cái kànjiàn Tiānmíng jíjímángmáng de pǎo guòlai
translation: Ten minutes later, they saw Tianming rushing over.

scenes.scene_goal: 表现张天明终于出现，并且非常匆忙。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头跟随张天明，他正气喘吁吁地跑在街上，跑向KTV的方向。他的朋友们在远处看到了他跑来的身影。
scenes.setting_en: A city street at night, near the KTV.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Tracking shot of Zhang Tianming running frantically down a street at night, towards the KTV. He is out of breath and has an apologetic look on his face. From the friends' point of view, they see him approaching from a distance.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 十分钟以后,才看见天明急急忙忙地跑过来。
voice_over.pinyin: shí fēnzhōng yǐhòu cái kànjiàn Tiānmíng jíjímángmáng de pǎo guòlai
voice_over.translation: Ten minutes later, they saw Tianming rushing over.

hanzi: 对不起,对不起,我在网上下载了一个软件,又查了一点儿东西,结果忘了时间了。
pinyin: duìbuqǐ duìbuqǐ wǒ zài wǎngshàng xiàzài le yí ge ruǎnjiàn yòu chá le yìdiǎnr dōngxi jiéguǒ wàng le shíjiān le
translation: Sorry, sorry. I was downloading some software and looking for some information online. As a result, I forgot the time.

scenes.scene_goal: 张天明向朋友们道歉并解释迟到原因。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 张天明跑到朋友们面前，双手合十，一边喘气一边连声道歉，解释自己因为上网而忘了时间。丽莎抱着手臂，面无表情。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Zhang Tianming arrives in front of his friends, panting. He puts his hands together in a pleading gesture and speaks apologetically. Lisha stands with her arms crossed, looking annoyed. Ke Lin and Xuemei look on.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 对不起,对不起,我在网上下载了一个软件,又查了一点儿东西,结果忘了时间了。
voice_over.pinyin: duìbuqǐ duìbuqǐ wǒ zài wǎngshàng xiàzài le yí ge ruǎnjiàn yòu chá le yìdiǎnr dōngxi jiéguǒ wàng le shíjiān le
voice_over.translation: Sorry, sorry. I was downloading some software and looking for some information online. As a result, I forgot the time.

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎追问，表达她的怀疑和不满。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 丽莎抱着手臂，没好气地质问天明。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium close-up on Lisha. She has her arms crossed and asks a question with a skeptical and annoyed expression.

hanzi: 我要写一篇文章,查一些资料。
pinyin: wǒ yào xiě yì piān wénzhāng chá yìxiē zīliào
translation: I need to write an article, so I was looking for some information.

scenes.scene_goal: 张天明进一步解释他迟到的“正当”理由。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 张天明认真地回答丽莎，解释自己是在为写文章查资料。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium close-up on Zhang Tianming. He looks earnest as he explains why he was looking for information online.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 我要写一篇文章,查一些资料。
voice_over.pinyin: wǒ yào xiě yì piān wénzhāng chá yìxiē zīliào
voice_over.translation: I need to write an article, so I was looking for some information.

hanzi: 查资料?我们的教授不让我们用网上的东西,一定要用书或者杂志。他说网上的垃圾太多了。
pinyin: chá zīliào wǒmen de jiàoshòu bù ràng wǒmen yòng wǎngshàng de dōngxi yídìng yào yòng shū huòzhě zázhì tā shuō wǎngshàng de lājī tài duō le
translation: Looking for information? Our professor doesn't let us use things from the internet. We have to use books or magazines. He says there's too much trash online.

scenes.scene_goal: 雪梅提出一个关于网络信息可靠性的不同观点。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 雪梅带着一丝担忧插话，她提到自己教授的观点，认为网上的信息不可靠。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium close-up on Xuemei. She looks concerned and shakes her head slightly as she talks about her professor's opinion on using online sources.

voice_over.speaker: 雪梅
voice_over.hanzi: 查资料?我们的教授不让我们用网上的东西,一定要用书或者杂志。他说网上的垃圾太多了。
voice_over.pinyin: chá zīliào wǒmen de jiàoshòu bù ràng wǒmen yòng wǎngshàng de dōngxi yídìng yào yòng shū huòzhě zázhì tā shuō wǎngshàng de lājī tài duō le
voice_over.translation: Looking for information? Our professor doesn't let us use things from the internet. We have to use books or magazines. He says there's too much trash online.

hanzi: 你的教授太落伍了,网上有很多资料很可靠很有用啊。网络世界又大又方便,你可以叫外卖、购物,你可以租房子、买车,衣食住行,什么都查得到。
pinyin: nǐ de jiàoshòu tài luòwǔ le wǎngshàng yǒu hěn duō zīliào hěn kěkào hěn yǒu yòng a wǎngluò shìjiè yòu dà yòu fāngbiàn nǐ kěyǐ jiào wàimài gòuwù nǐ kěyǐ zū fángzi mǎi chē yī shí zhù xíng shénme dōu chá de dào
translation: Your professor is too outdated. There's a lot of reliable and useful information online. The online world is so big and convenient. You can order takeout, shop, rent an apartment, buy a car; you can find anything for your basic needs.

scenes.scene_goal: 柯林反驳雪梅的观点，并热情地赞美网络的便利。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 柯林笑着反驳雪梅，他充满激情地挥着手，讲述网络的各种好处。随着他的话语，屏幕上可以快速闪现外卖、购物、租房网站的界面图标。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Ke Lin laughs and steps forward, speaking enthusiastically and gesturing with his hands. He defends the internet. As he mentions takeout, shopping, and renting, small, animated icons for these activities pop up briefly around him.

voice_over.speaker: 柯林
voice_over.hanzi: 你的教授太落伍了,网上有很多资料很可靠很有用啊。网络世界又大又方便,你可以叫外卖、购物,你可以租房子、买车,衣食住行,什么都查得到。
voice_over.pinyin: nǐ de jiàoshòu tài luòwǔ le wǎngshàng yǒu hěn duō zīliào hěn kěkào hěn yǒu yòng a wǎngluò shìjiè yòu dà yòu fāngbiàn nǐ kěyǐ jiào wàimài gòuwù nǐ kěyǐ zū fángzi mǎi chē yī shí zhù xíng shénme dōu chá de dào
voice_over.translation: Your professor is too outdated. There's a lot of reliable and useful information online. The online world is so big and convenient. You can order takeout, shop, rent an apartment, buy a car; you can find anything for your basic needs.

hanzi: 对,特别方便。要是想交女朋友,也可以上网找。
pinyin: duì tèbié fāngbiàn yàoshi xiǎng jiāo nǚpéngyou yě kěyǐ shàng wǎng zhǎo
translation: Right, it's especially convenient. If you want to find a girlfriend, you can also look online.

scenes.scene_goal: 柯林开玩笑，进一步强调网络的万能。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 柯林对张天明挤了挤眼，开玩笑说甚至可以在网上找女朋友。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.shot_type: Two-shot of Ke Lin and Zhang Tianming.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Ke Lin agrees with himself and then turns to Zhang Tianming, winking playfully as he suggests finding a girlfriend online.

voice_over.speaker: 柯林
voice_over.hanzi: 对,特别方便。要是想交女朋友,也可以上网找。
voice_over.pinyin: duì tèbié fāngbiàn yàoshi xiǎng jiāo nǚpéngyou yě kěyǐ shàng wǎng zhǎo
voice_over.translation: Right, it's especially convenient. If you want to find a girlfriend, you can also look online.

hanzi: 哎,丽莎,你还在生我的气吗?
pinyin: āi Lìshā nǐ hái zài shēng wǒ de qì ma
translation: Hey, Lisha, are you still mad at me?

scenes.scene_goal: 张天明试图缓和气氛，将注意力转回仍在生气的丽莎身上。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 张天明没有理会柯林的玩笑，他小心翼翼地转向仍然板着脸的丽莎，试探性地问她是否还在生气。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.shot_type: Over-the-shoulder shot from behind Lisha, focusing on Tianming's worried face.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Zhang Tianming ignores Ke Lin's joke and turns his attention back to Lisha, who is still visibly upset. He asks her cautiously if she is still angry, his face full of concern.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 哎,丽莎,你还在生我的气吗?
voice_over.pinyin: āi Lìshā nǐ hái zài shēng wǒ de qì ma
voice_over.translation: Hey, Lisha, are you still mad at me?

hanzi: 你说我该不该生气?你每次都迟到,老是害得大家等你。
pinyin: nǐ shuō wǒ gāi bù gāi shēngqì nǐ měi cì dōu chídào lǎoshì hàide dàjiā děng nǐ
translation: You tell me if I should be angry. You're late every time, always making everyone wait for you.

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎终于爆发，说出了她生气的真正原因。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 丽莎终于忍不住了，她对着张天明发火，指责他总是迟到，让大家等他。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Close-up on Lisha's face as she finally vents her frustration at Zhang Tianming, her expression is angry and hurt.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 你说我该不该生气?你每次都迟到,老是害得大家等你。
voice_over.pinyin: nǐ shuō wǒ gāi bù gāi shēngqì nǐ měi cì dōu chídào lǎoshì hàide dàjiā děng nǐ
voice_over.translation: You tell me if I should be angry. You're late every time, always making everyone wait for you.

hanzi: 对不起,对不起,是我不好。下次不敢了。
pinyin: duìbuqǐ duìbuqǐ shì wǒ bù hǎo xià cì bù gǎn le
translation: Sorry, sorry, it's my fault. I won't dare to do it again.

scenes.scene_goal: 张天明再次真诚道歉，承认错误。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 面对丽莎的指责，张天明低下头，非常诚恳地道歉，并保证下次不会再犯。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Zhang Tianming looks down, looking genuinely remorseful. He apologizes sincerely, promising it won't happen again.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 对不起,对不起,是我不好。下次不敢了。
voice_over.pinyin: duìbuqǐ duìbuqǐ shì wǒ bù hǎo xià cì bù gǎn le
voice_over.translation: Sorry, sorry, it's my fault. I won't dare to do it again.

hanzi: 天明,你整天待在屋子里玩儿电脑,看起来真是玩儿上瘾了。
pinyin: Tiānmíng nǐ zhěngtiān dāi zài wūzi li wánr diànnǎo kàn qǐlai zhēnshi wánr shàngyǐn le
translation: Tianming, you stay in your room all day playing on the computer. It really seems like you're addicted.

scenes.scene_goal: 柯林点出问题的核心：张天明玩电脑上瘾了。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 柯林走上前，拍了拍天明的肩膀，用一种既是关心又是调侃的语气说他看起来像是玩电脑上瘾了。镜头最后停在天明若有所思的表情上。
scenes.setting_en: The flashy, neon-lit entrance of a Karaoke (KTV) bar at night.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Ke Lin puts a hand on Zhang Tianming's shoulder and speaks with a mix of concern and friendly teasing. The scene ends with a shot of Zhang Tianming looking thoughtful as his friend's words sink in.

voice_over.speaker: 柯林
voice_over.hanzi: 天明,你整天待在屋子里玩儿电脑,看起来真是玩儿上瘾了。
voice_over.pinyin: Tiānmíng nǐ zhěngtiān dāi zài wūzi li wánr diànnǎo kàn qǐlai zhēnshi wánr shàngyǐn le
voice_over.translation: Tianming, you stay in your room all day playing on the computer. It really seems like you're addicted.

scenes.scene_goal: 雪梅引入新词“电聊”，制造悬念。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 场景在咖啡馆。雪梅身体前倾，带着神秘又得意的笑容，对大家说自己喜欢“电聊”。丽莎和柯林看着她，似乎也很好奇。
scenes.setting_en: Cozy coffee shop interior, daytime. Four friends at a table.
scenes.camera_movement: Slow zoom in on Xuemei's face.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Slice-of-life anime style. A cheerful Chinese girl (Xuemei) in a coffee shop leans forward with a playful, mysterious smile, speaking to her friends. The focus is on her expressive face.

scenes.scene_goal: 张天明对新词感到困惑，并提出疑问。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头切到张天明，他皱着眉头，满脸困惑地看着雪梅，重复着“电聊”这个词并追问。
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot, focus pull from Xuemei in the background to Tianming in the foreground.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Slice-of-life anime style. Close-up on a confused young Chinese man (Tianming) with glasses in a coffee shop. He furrows his brow and asks a question, looking at his friend off-screen.

hanzi: 就是打“电”话“聊”天儿。懂了吧?一直聊、不停地聊、从早到晚地聊……
pinyin: jiùshì dǎ diàn huà liáo tiānr dǒng le ba? yìzhí liáo bùtíng de liáo cóng zǎo dào wǎn de liáo
translation: It's 'tele'phone 'chat'ting. Get it? Chatting continuously, non-stop, from morning till night...

scenes.scene_goal: 雪梅解释“电聊”的含义，并夸张地描述其状态，为丽莎的误解做铺垫。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 雪梅笑着解释，并用手比作打电话的姿势，做出滔滔不绝、聊个不停的夸张表情和动作。画面可以快速闪过时钟飞速旋转、太阳和月亮交替的蒙太奇效果，来表现“从早到晚”。
scenes.setting_en: Cozy coffee shop interior, with a brief montage effect.
scenes.camera_movement: Start on a medium shot of Xuemei, then cut to a quick montage sequence, then back to Xuemei's triumphant expression.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Slice-of-life anime style. The cheerful Chinese girl (Xuemei) explains something with exaggerated hand gestures, mimicking talking on a phone. A quick montage shows a clock spinning fast, sun and moon rising and falling, to illustrate 'from morning till night'. She looks proud of her explanation.

voice_over.speaker: 雪梅
voice_over.hanzi: 就是打“电”话“聊”天儿。懂了吧?一直聊、不停地聊、从早到晚地聊……
voice_over.pinyin: jiùshì dǎ diàn huà liáo tiānr dǒng le ba? yìzhí liáo bùtíng de liáo cóng zǎo dào wǎn de liáo
voice_over.translation: It's 'tele'phone 'chat'ting. Get it? Chatting continuously, non-stop, from morning till night...

hanzi: 怎么?听起来好像你不爱跟我聊天儿。
pinyin: zěnme? tīngqilai hǎoxiàng nǐ bú ài gēn wǒ liáotiānr
translation: What? It sounds like you don't like chatting with me.

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎误解了雪梅和天明的对话，感觉自己被影射，气氛变得有些尴尬。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 听完雪梅的描述，丽莎的笑容消失了。她放下咖啡杯，撅起嘴，带着一丝委屈和不满看着张天明，质问他。
scenes.camera_movement: Rack focus from Xuemei and Tianming laughing to Lisha's slightly hurt expression.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Slice-of-life anime style. A blonde Western girl (Lisha) in a coffee shop, her smile fades. She looks hurt and slightly annoyed, questioning her friend (Tianming) who is off-screen. The mood shifts from cheerful to slightly awkward.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 怎么?听起来好像你不爱跟我聊天儿。
voice_over.pinyin: zěnme? tīngqilai hǎoxiàng nǐ bú ài gēn wǒ liáotiānr
voice_over.translation: What? It sounds like you don't like chatting with me.

hanzi: 不,不,不,我不是那个意思,我是开玩笑。我当然爱跟你聊天儿。好了,好了,不说这些了,我们到底去哪家卡拉OK啊?
pinyin: bù, bù, bù, wǒ bú shì nà ge yì si, wǒ shì kāi wán xiào. wǒ dāng rán ài gēn nǐ liáo tiānr. hǎo le, hǎo le, bù shuō zhè xiē le, wǒ men dào dǐ qù nǎ jiā kǎ lā OK a?
translation: No, no, no, that's not what I mean. I was joking. Of course I love chatting with you. Alright, alright, let's not talk about this anymore. Which karaoke place are we going to after all?

scenes.scene_goal: 张天明立刻澄清误会，安抚丽莎，并迅速转移话题以化解尴尬。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 张天明见状立刻慌了，连忙摆手否认，表情真诚地向丽莎解释。为了打破僵局，他话锋一转，兴奋地提议去唱卡拉OK。
scenes.camera_movement: Quick zoom in on Tianming's flustered face as he waves his hands, then pull back to a two-shot as he tries to change the subject.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Slice-of-life anime style. The Chinese man (Tianming) looks flustered, waving his hands frantically to deny something. He then smiles reassuringly at his friend (Lisha) and eagerly suggests a new activity to change the subject.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 不,不,不,我不是那个意思,我是开玩笑。我当然爱跟你聊天儿。好了,好了,不说这些了,我们到底去哪家卡拉OK啊?
voice_over.pinyin: bù, bù, bù, wǒ bú shì nà ge yì si, wǒ shì kāi wán xiào. wǒ dāng rán ài gēn nǐ liáo tiānr. hǎo le, hǎo le, bù shuō zhè xiē le, wǒ men dào dǐ qù nǎ jiā kǎ lā OK a?
voice_over.translation: No, no, no, that's not what I mean. I was joking. Of course I love chatting with you. Alright, alright, let's not talk about this anymore. Which karaoke place are we going to after all?

hanzi: 我上网查了,东边儿那家不错,那儿的歌都是丽莎喜欢的。你们看,我没忘了朋友吧?
pinyin: wǒ shàng wǎng chá le, dōng bianr nà jiā bú cuò, nàr de gē dōu shì Lì shā xǐ huan de. nǐ men kàn, wǒ méi wàng le péng you ba?
translation: I looked it up online. That place on the east side is pretty good, and they have all the songs Lisha likes. See, I didn't forget my friends, did I?

scenes.scene_goal: 张天明用实际行动证明他关心朋友，彻底化解了误会，气氛重归于好。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 张天明拿出手机，展示他查好的卡拉OK店信息，屏幕上显示着店名和一些歌曲列表。他特意把手机转向丽莎，向她和大家证明自己心里有朋友。丽莎看到后，终于破涕为笑，朋友们也都开心地笑了起来。
scenes.camera_movement: Close-up on the smartphone screen showing a karaoke website. Pan up to Tianming's proud face, then cut to a wide shot of the whole group smiling, with Lisha looking happy and relieved.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Slice-of-life anime style. Close-up on a smartphone screen showing a karaoke venue's webpage. A young Chinese man (Tianming) proudly shows his phone to his friends. The blonde girl (Lisha) who was upset before now breaks into a happy smile. The whole group looks cheerful again.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 我上网查了,东边儿那家不错,那儿的歌都是丽莎喜欢的。你们看,我没忘了朋友吧?
voice_over.pinyin: wǒ shàng wǎng chá le, dōng bianr nà jiā bú cuò, nàr de gē dōu shì Lì shā xǐ huan de. nǐ men kàn, wǒ méi wàng le péng you ba?
voice_over.translation: I looked it up online. That place on the east side is pretty good, and they have all the songs Lisha likes. See, I didn't forget my friends, did I?

global_config.teaching_style: A clear, structured, and encouraging guide for foundational Chinese learners. The approach breaks down dialogues sentence by sentence, focusing on vocabulary and immediate context before moving to complex grammar.
global_config.visual_style: Clean, modern, and minimalist, using animated text and simple icons on cards to illustrate concepts.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the introduction of Zhang Tianming and learn how to describe someone who is obsessed with something.

narration.subtitle_en: The dialogue begins by introducing a character named Zhang Tianming. The sentence says [zh:张天明是个电脑迷]. The key term here is [zh:电脑迷], which means 'computer fanatic' or 'computer geek'. The character [zh:迷] means 'fan' or 'enthusiast'. You can attach it to other nouns, like [zh:球迷] for a sports fan. The second part, [zh:他的电脑从早到晚都开着], means 'his computer is on from morning till night'. [zh:从早到晚] is a useful phrase for 'from morning to night'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 张天明是个电脑迷,他的电脑从早到晚都开着。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng shì ge diànnǎo mí, tā de diànnǎo cóng zǎo dào wǎn dōu kāi zhe.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Zhang Tianming is a computer fanatic; his computer is on from morning till night.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A person who is obsessed with computers. '迷' means 'fan' or 'enthusiast'.
A common time phrase indicating an action lasts all day.

segments.visual_notes: Animate an icon of a person glued to a computer screen. Highlight the characters '电脑迷'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for common online activities.

narration.subtitle_en: This part describes what Tianming does online. It lists several activities: [zh:看新闻] 'read news', [zh:查资料] 'look for information', [zh:玩儿游戏] 'play games', and [zh:写博客] 'write on his blog'. Notice the pattern: a verb followed by a noun. This is a great way to build your vocabulary for daily activities. The word [zh:网站] means 'website'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他在网上看新闻、查资料、玩儿游戏,有时候还在自己的网站上写博客,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā zài wǎngshàng kàn xīnwén, chá zīliào, wánr yóuxì, yǒushíhou hái zài zìjǐ de wǎngzhàn shàng xiě bókè,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: He reads news, looks for information, and plays games online. Sometimes he also writes on his blog,

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for news, documents, a game controller, and a blog, each appearing as the corresponding word is spoken.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the emphatic negative phrase '一会儿都离不开'.

narration.subtitle_en: The sentence concludes with [zh:一会儿都离不开电脑]. This means 'he can't tear himself away from the computer, not even for a moment'. The key phrase is [zh:一会儿都离不开]. [zh:一会儿] means 'a moment', and when used with [zh:都] and a negative verb like [zh:离不开], it creates an emphatic negation. It strongly expresses his inability to leave the computer, suggesting an addiction or deep absorption.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 一会儿都离不开电脑。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yīhuìr dōu lí bù kāi diànnǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: He can't tear himself away from his computer for a moment.
on_screen_text.notes: Structure: 一会儿都 + 不/没 + Verb

highlight_words.translation: can't leave, can't be separated from
highlight_words.explanation_en: A potential complement indicating the inability to be separated from something or someone.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the phrase '一会儿都离不开' and show a visual of a person stuck to their computer chair.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the '一...就...' structure for sequential actions.

narration.subtitle_en: Here we see a common pattern: [zh:一...就...]. The sentence is [zh:他常常一上网,就忘了时间]. This means 'As soon as he goes online, he forgets the time'. The [zh:一...就...] structure connects two actions that happen one right after the other. The first action is [zh:上网] 'to go online', and the immediate result is [zh:忘了时间] 'to forget the time'. It's a very useful pattern to describe cause and effect or a sequence of events.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他常常一上网,就忘了时间。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā chángcháng yī shàng wǎng, jiù wàng le shíjiān.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: As soon as he goes online, he loses track of time.
on_screen_text.notes: Structure: 一 + Action 1, 就 + Action 2

segments.visual_notes: Animate the character '一' appearing before '上网' and '就' appearing before '忘了时间' to visually link them.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the setup for the conflict: a missed social event.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence sets up the story's conflict. It starts with [zh:昨天], 'yesterday'. His friends had arranged to go sing karaoke, or [zh:唱卡拉OK]. The phrase [zh:约好] means 'to have arranged' or 'to have agreed on a plan'. But when the time came, [zh:时间到了], he didn't show up: [zh:他也没来]. This part of the story establishes why his friends are looking for him.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 昨天,丽莎、柯林、雪梅和他约好今天一起去唱卡拉OK,时间到了他也没来,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zuótiān, Lìshā, Kē Lín, Xuěméi hé tā yuē hǎo jīntiān yìqǐ qù chàng kǎlā'ōukēi, shíjiān dào le tā yě méi lái,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Yesterday, Lisha, Ke Lin, Xuemei, and he had arranged to go to karaoke today. When the time came, he didn't show up,

segments.visual_notes: Show a calendar icon for 'yesterday' and a microphone icon for 'karaoke'. Then show a clock with the hands spinning, and a character not appearing.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn phrases for trying to contact someone and the result.

narration.subtitle_en: His friends tried to reach him. First, [zh:打他的手机], 'they called his cell phone'. But, [zh:也不接], 'he didn't answer'. The verb [zh:接] here means 'to pick up' a call. Since calling didn't work, they tried another method: [zh:就给他发了一个电子邮件], 'so they sent him an email'. [zh:电子邮件] is the word for 'email'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 打他的手机,也不接,就给他发了一个电子邮件。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: dǎ tā de shǒujī, yě bù jiē, jiù gěi tā fā le yí ge diànzǐ yóujiàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: They called his cell phone, but he didn't answer, so they sent him an email.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a ringing phone with a red 'X' over it, followed by an icon of an email being sent.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the adverb '急急忙忙' and the directional complement '跑过来'.

narration.subtitle_en: Finally, Tianming appears. [zh:十分钟以后], 'ten minutes later'. The word [zh:急急忙忙地] is an adverb that vividly describes doing something in a hurry. He was [zh:跑过来], 'running over'. The directional complement [zh:过来] indicates movement towards the speaker. Since his friends are the ones telling the story, his running is 'coming over' to them. This shows the email worked!

on_screen_text.focus_text: 十分钟以后,才看见天明急急忙忙地跑过来。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: shí fēnzhōng yǐhòu, cái kànjiàn Tiānmíng jíjímángmáng de pǎo guòlai.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Ten minutes later, they saw Tianming rushing over.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Often reduplicated as '急急忙忙' for emphasis.
'跑' is to run, and '过来' indicates direction towards the speaker.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a character running quickly towards the screen. Highlight the word '急急忙忙'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to apologize and give an excuse using '结果'.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming apologizes by saying [zh:对不起,对不起]. He then explains why he was late. He says he was [zh:下载了一个软件], 'downloading a piece of software', and [zh:又查了一点儿东西], 'and also looked up some information'. The key word here is [zh:结果], which means 'as a result'. It connects his actions to the outcome: [zh:结果忘了时间了], 'as a result, I forgot the time'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对不起,对不起,我在网上下载了一个软件,又查了一点儿东西,结果忘了时间了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duìbuqǐ, duìbuqǐ, wǒ zài wǎngshàng xiàzài le yí ge ruǎnjiàn, yòu chá le yìdiǎnr dōngxi, jiéguǒ wàng le shíjiān le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Sorry, sorry. I was downloading some software and looking for some information online. As a result, I forgot the time.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon for downloading, then an arrow pointing to a clock with spinning hands, with the word '结果' on the arrow.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the words for 'software' and 'hardware' and their regional variations.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's look at the word for 'software', [zh:软件]. It's composed of [zh:软], meaning 'soft', and [zh:件], meaning 'item' or 'piece'. So, literally, a 'soft item'. This makes it easy to remember its counterpart, 'hardware', which is [zh:硬件]. Here, [zh:硬] means 'hard'. It's worth noting that in Taiwan, different terms are used: [zh:软体] for software and [zh:硬体] for hardware.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 软件 / 硬件
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: ruǎnjiàn / yìngjiàn
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: software / hardware
on_screen_text.notes: Taiwan variants: 软体 (ruăntǐ) / 硬体 (yìngtǐ)

segments.visual_notes: Display '软件' with a soft, flexible icon and '硬件' with a solid, rigid icon. Add a small map icon to show the regional variations.

segments.teaching_goal: To recognize and understand a simple follow-up question.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a very short and direct question from one of the friends. [zh:查什么?] means 'Looking for what?'. The verb [zh:查] means 'to check' or 'to look up', and [zh:什么] is the question word for 'what'. It's a natural follow-up to Tianming's excuse that he was looking for something online.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 查什么?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Chá shénme?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Looking for what?

segments.visual_notes: A large question mark appears next to the text.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to state a purpose for an action.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming answers the question. He says, [zh:我要写一篇文章,查一些资料]. This means 'I need to write an article, so I was looking for some information'. [zh:文章] is the word for 'article' or 'essay'. The measure word for articles is [zh:篇]. So, 'one article' is [zh:一篇文章]. He states his goal first—writing the article—and then the action he was taking to achieve it—looking for information.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我要写一篇文章,查一些资料。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ yào xiě yì piān wénzhāng, chá yìxiē zīliào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I need to write an article, so I was looking for some information.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a pen writing on paper, which then transforms into a document icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a negative command and a requirement using '不让' and '一定'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, another friend, Xuemei, expresses surprise. She says her professor has a strict rule: [zh:不让我们用网上的东西]. This means 'doesn't let us use things from the internet'. The verb [zh:让] means 'to let' or 'to allow'. The rule is that they [zh:一定要用书或者杂志], 'must use books or magazines'. [zh:一定] means 'must' or 'definitely', expressing a strong requirement.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 查资料?我们的教授不让我们用网上的东西,一定要用书或者杂志。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Chá zīliào? Wǒmen de jiàoshòu bù ràng wǒmen yòng wǎngshàng de dōngxi, yídìng yào yòng shū huòzhě zázhì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Looking for information? Our professor doesn't let us use things from the internet. We have to use books or magazines.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of the internet with a red 'X' over it, and a green checkmark over icons of a book and a magazine.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the word '垃圾' and understand the professor's reasoning.

narration.subtitle_en: Xuemei explains her professor's reasoning: [zh:他说网上的垃圾太多了]. This means, 'He says there's too much trash online'. The key word here is [zh:垃圾], which means 'trash' or 'junk'. In this context, it refers to unreliable or low-quality information. It's a strong word to express disapproval of the quality of online content.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他说网上的垃圾太多了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā shuō wǎngshàng de lājī tài duō le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: He says there's too much trash online.

segments.visual_notes: Show a computer screen filled with trash can icons.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the word '垃圾' and its regional pronunciation difference.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's focus on the word [zh:垃圾]. In Mainland China, it's pronounced [zh:lājī]. However, if you're in Taiwan, you'll hear it pronounced as 'lèsè'. It's the same word with the same meaning, just a regional difference in pronunciation. This is a common phenomenon in Chinese, so it's good to be aware of it.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 垃圾
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: lājī
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: trash, garbage
on_screen_text.notes:
Pronunciation in Mainland China: lājī
Pronunciation in Taiwan: lèsè

highlight_words.explanation_en: Pronounced 'lājī' in Mainland China and 'lèsè' in Taiwan.

segments.visual_notes: Show a map of China and Taiwan, with the different pinyin appearing over each region.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to disagree and provide counterarguments.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming disagrees with the professor's view. He says, [zh:你的教授太落伍了]. [zh:落伍] means 'outdated' or 'behind the times'. He argues that [zh:网上有很多资料很可靠很有用啊]. This means 'there's a lot of information online that is very reliable and very useful'. [zh:可靠] means 'reliable', and [zh:有用] means 'useful'. He is directly countering the idea that everything online is 'trash'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你的教授太落伍了,网上有很多资料很可靠很有用啊。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ de jiàoshòu tài luòwǔ le, wǎngshàng yǒu hěn duō zīliào hěn kěkào hěn yǒu yòng a.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Your professor is too outdated. There's a lot of reliable and useful information online.

segments.visual_notes: Show an old-fashioned professor icon, then transition to a modern, glowing internet icon. Highlight '可靠' and '有用'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the word '网络' and its regional variation.

narration.subtitle_en: The word for 'internet' or 'network' is [zh:网络]. It's made of two characters: [zh:网] which means 'net', and [zh:络] which means 'network' or 'to connect'. So, it literally means 'net-work'. Similar to our previous example, this word also has a regional variation. In Taiwan, people usually say [zh:网路] instead of [zh:网络]. [zh:路] means 'road', so you can think of it as an 'information road'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 网络
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: wǎngluò
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: network, the Internet

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of a net ('网') connecting different points. Then show a map highlighting Taiwan with the word '网路'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for various services available online.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming continues to list the benefits of the internet. He says the [zh:网络世界又大又方便], 'the online world is both big and convenient'. He gives examples of what you can do: [zh:叫外卖] 'order takeout', [zh:购物] 'go shopping', [zh:租房子] 'rent an apartment', and [zh:买车] 'buy a car'. These are all examples of the internet's convenience in modern life.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 网络世界又大又方便,你可以叫外卖、购物,你可以租房子、买车,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǎngluò shìjiè yòu dà yòu fāngbiàn, nǐ kěyǐ jiào wàimài, gòuwù, nǐ kěyǐ zū fángzi, mǎi chē,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The online world is so big and convenient. You can order takeout, shop, rent an apartment, buy a car,

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for takeout food, a shopping cart, a house for rent, and a car, appearing in sequence.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the idiom '衣食住行' and the potential complement '查得到'.

narration.subtitle_en: He summarizes by saying [zh:衣食住行,什么都查得到]. [zh:衣食住行] is a four-character idiom that means 'clothing, food, housing, and transportation'—the basic necessities of life. He's saying you can find anything related to these online. The phrase [zh:查得到] is a potential complement. It means 'to be able to find (by searching)'. The [zh:得] indicates that the action of searching, [zh:查], can be successfully completed.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 衣食住行,什么都查得到。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yī shí zhù xíng, shénme dōu chá de dào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: clothing, food, housing, and transportation—you can find anything.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally: clothing, food, housing, transportation.
A potential complement indicating a successful outcome is possible.

segments.visual_notes: Display the four characters of '衣食住行' with icons for each (a shirt, a bowl of rice, a house, a car).

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a conditional sentence with '要是' and a humorous use case for the internet.

narration.subtitle_en: Ke Lin chimes in, agreeing with Tianming. He says [zh:对,特别方便], 'Right, it's especially convenient'. Then he adds a humorous example: [zh:要是想交女朋友,也可以上网找]. This is a conditional sentence starting with [zh:要是], which means 'if'. The sentence means, 'If you want to find a girlfriend, you can also look online'. This lightens the mood of the conversation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对,特别方便。要是想交女朋友,也可以上网找。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duì, tèbié fāngbiàn. Yàoshi xiǎng jiāo nǚpéngyou, yě kěyǐ shàng wǎng zhǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Right, it's especially convenient. If you want to find a girlfriend, you can also look online.

segments.visual_notes: Show a heart icon on a computer screen to illustrate the idea of online dating.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the phrase '生(谁)的气' for being angry at someone.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming senses that Lisha is still upset about him being late. He tries to change the subject and address her directly. He says, [zh:哎,丽莎,你还在生我的气吗?]. This means, 'Hey, Lisha, are you still mad at me?'. The key phrase is [zh:生我的气]. The structure is '生 + person's + 气'. For example, 'to be mad at him' is [zh:生他的气]. It's a very common way to talk about being angry with someone.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哎,丽莎,你还在生我的气吗?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Āi, Lìshā, nǐ hái zài shēng wǒ de qì ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Hey, Lisha, are you still mad at me?
on_screen_text.notes: Structure: 生 + [Person's] + 气 (to be mad at [Person])

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'to give birth to anger'. Used in the pattern '生(谁)的气' to mean 'to be angry at (someone)'.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of Tianming turning towards Lisha, with a speech bubble containing the question. An angry emoji appears over Lisha's head.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a rhetorical question with '该不该' and the verb '害得'.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha responds with a rhetorical question: [zh:你说我该不该生气?], 'You tell me if I should be angry or not?'. [zh:该] is short for [zh:应该], meaning 'should'. The 'A-not-A' form [zh:该不该] creates the question. She then explains why she's angry: [zh:你每次都迟到], 'You're late every time', and [zh:老是害得大家等你], 'always making everyone wait for you'. The word [zh:害得] implies that his action caused a negative consequence for others.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你说我该不该生气?你每次都迟到,老是害得大家等你。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ shuō wǒ gāi bù gāi shēngqì? Nǐ měi cì dōu chídào, lǎoshì hài de dàjiā děng nǐ.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: You tell me if I should be angry. You're late every time, always making everyone wait for you.

highlight_words.translation: to cause trouble, to harm so that...

segments.visual_notes: Show a group of people looking at their watches impatiently. Highlight the word '害得'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn phrases for taking responsibility and making a promise.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming apologizes again, this time more sincerely. He says [zh:对不起,对不起,是我不好]. The phrase [zh:是我不好] is a great way to take the blame, meaning 'It's my fault' or 'I was wrong'. He then makes a promise for the future: [zh:下次不敢了]. This literally means 'Next time, I won't dare', but it's a common, slightly informal way to say 'It won't happen again'. It shows he understands his mistake.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对不起,对不起,是我不好。下次不敢了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duìbuqǐ, duìbuqǐ, shì wǒ bù hǎo. Xiàcì bù gǎn le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Sorry, sorry, it's my fault. I won't dare to do it again.

highlight_words.translation: I won't do it again. (Lit: Next time I won't dare.)

segments.visual_notes: Show Tianming bowing his head slightly in apology. Highlight the phrases '是我不好' and '下次不敢了'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express that someone seems addicted to something.

narration.subtitle_en: The dialogue begins with a friend observing Tianming. She says, [zh:你整天待在屋子里玩儿电脑]. Let's break this down. [zh:整天] means 'all day long.' [zh:待在] means 'to stay at' or 'to be in.' [zh:屋子里] means 'in the room.' The main point is at the end: [zh:看起来真是玩儿上瘾了], which means 'it looks like you're really addicted to playing.' The key phrase here is [zh:上瘾了], meaning 'to be addicted.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 天明,你整天待在屋子里玩儿电脑,看起来真是玩儿上瘾了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tiānmíng, nǐ zhěngtiān dāi zài wūzi lǐ wánr diànnǎo, kàn qǐlái zhēnshi wánr shàngyǐn le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Tianming, you stay in your room all day playing on the computer. It really seems like you're addicted.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb meaning 'the entire day'. It's composed of 整 (zhěng), meaning 'whole' or 'entire', and 天 (tiān), meaning 'day'.
A verb phrase indicating staying in a particular location. 待 (dāi) means 'to stay'.
A verb-object compound. 上 (shàng) can mean 'to get into a state', and 瘾 (yǐn) means 'addiction'. So, it literally means 'to get into an addiction'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a character sitting at a computer from morning to night, with a clock spinning fast.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to downplay an accusation and provide a justification.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming responds defensively. He starts by questioning the accusation: [zh:上瘾? 没那么严重吧?] This means 'Addicted? It's not that serious, is it?' [zh:严重] means 'serious,' and the particle [zh:吧] softens the tone. He then justifies his behavior by saying, [zh:现在是网络时代,当然离不开电脑]. This means, 'This is the internet age, of course you can't be without a computer.' The key phrase [zh:离不开] expresses an inability to be separated from something, suggesting it's a necessity.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 上瘾?没那么严重吧?现在是网络时代,当然离不开电脑。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shàngyǐn? Méi nàme yánzhòng ba? Xiànzài shì wǎngluò shídài, dāngrán líbukāi diànnǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Addicted? It's not that serious, is it? This is the internet age, of course you can't be without a computer.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adjective used to describe the gravity of a situation, illness, or problem.
A noun phrase combining 网络 (wǎngluò), 'internet', and 时代 (shídài), 'era' or 'age'.

highlight_words.translation: cannot do without, cannot be separated from
highlight_words.explanation_en: A potential complement indicating that separation is not possible, either due to practical need or emotional attachment.

segments.visual_notes: Show a globe covered in a network grid, with icons for various internet activities.

segments.segment_title: Line 13, Part 1: The benefits of the internet
segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to list the positive uses of computers and the internet.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa acknowledges the importance of computers. She says, [zh:我知道电脑和网络在我们的生活中越来越重要]. This means 'I know computers and the internet are becoming more and more important in our lives.' The phrase [zh:越来越] means 'more and more.' She then lists its uses: [zh:我也用电脑帮助我做翻译练习], 'I also use the computer to help me with translation exercises,' and [zh:也上网比较价钱], 'and also go online to compare prices.' Notice the verb [zh:帮助] for 'to help'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我知道电脑和网络在我们的生活中越来越重要,我也用电脑帮助我做翻译练习,也上网比较价钱,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ zhīdào diànnǎo hé wǎngluò zài wǒmen de shēnghuó zhōng yuèláiyuè zhòngyào, wǒ yě yòng diànnǎo bāngzhù wǒ zuò fānyì liànxí, yě shàngwǎng bǐjiào jiàqián,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I know computers and the internet are becoming more and more important in our lives. I also use the computer to help me with my translation exercises, and I go online to compare prices,

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure used before an adjective or verb to indicate a progressive change over time.
A formal verb for 'to help'. It can directly take an object, e.g., 帮助我 (bāngzhù wǒ).
A verb meaning 'to compare'. It can also be used as an adverb meaning 'relatively' or 'fairly'.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for translation apps and online shopping sites.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express a contrast and point out negative consequences.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, Lisa contrasts her usage with Tianming's. She starts with [zh:可是不像你], which means 'but I'm not like you.' [zh:可是] is a common word for 'but,' and [zh:不像] means 'not like.' She then describes his behavior: [zh:常常忘了时间,忘了朋友]. This means 'always forgetting the time, forgetting friends.' The verb [zh:忘] means 'to forget.' The repetition emphasizes the negative consequences of his computer use.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 可是不像你,常常忘了时间,忘了朋友。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: kěshì bù xiàng nǐ, chángcháng wàng le shíjiān, wàng le péngyou.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: but I'm not like you, always forgetting the time and your friends.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to introduce a contrast or an opposing idea.
Used to state that something or someone is not similar to another.
A verb meaning to forget. It's often followed by 了 (le) to indicate completion.

segments.visual_notes: Show a character at a computer while a clock spins and an image of friends fades away.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to talk about different methods of digital communication.

narration.subtitle_en: Another friend joins in. He says, [zh:听丽莎说你不喜欢打电话]. The structure [zh:听...说] means 'I hear from...' or '...said that.' So, 'I hear from Lisha that you don't like to make phone calls.' He continues with Tianming's preference: [zh:只喜欢发微信、发电邮]. [zh:只] means 'only.' Notice the verb [zh:发], which means 'to send,' is used for both [zh:微信] (WeChat) and [zh:电邮] (email). In contrast, making a call uses the verb [zh:打].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 天明,听丽莎说你不喜欢打电话,只喜欢发微信、发电邮。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tiānmíng, tīng Lìshā shuō nǐ bù xǐhuān dǎ diànhuà, zhǐ xǐhuān fā wēixìn, fā diànyóu.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Tianming, I hear from Lisha that you don't like to make phone calls, you only like to send WeChat messages and emails.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The standard phrase for making a phone call. 打 (dǎ) means 'to hit' but has many other uses, including 'to make a call'.
发 (fā) means 'to send'. WeChat (微信) is a dominant messaging app in China.
发 (fā) means 'to send'. 电邮 (diànyóu) is a common abbreviation for 电子邮件 (diànzǐ yóujiàn), meaning 'email'.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for a phone call being crossed out, and icons for WeChat and email being highlighted.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to state that something is free.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming gives a very practical reason for his preference. He says, [zh:微信、电邮不花钱]. This is a short and direct sentence. It means 'WeChat and email don't cost money.' The key phrase is [zh:不花钱]. [zh:花钱] literally means 'to spend money,' so adding [zh:不] negates it, meaning it's free.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 微信、电邮不花钱。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wēixìn, diànyóu bù huā qián.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: WeChat and email don't cost money.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object phrase. 花 (huā) means 'to spend' and 钱 (qián) is 'money'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a wallet with money flying out, then show a big red cross over it.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to agree and list multiple positive attributes using '又...又...'.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming's friend agrees enthusiastically. He starts with [zh:没错!], which means 'That's right!' or 'Correct!'. He then says [zh:免费], which is a more formal word for 'free of charge.' Then he adds, [zh:又快又方便]. This is a very useful structure, 'yòu... yòu...', used to link two adjectives. Here, it means 'both fast and convenient.' [zh:快] is 'fast' and [zh:方便] is 'convenient'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 没错!免费,又快又方便。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Méi cuò! Miǎnfèi, yòu kuài yòu fāngbiàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: That's right! They're free, and also fast and convenient.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'not wrong'. A common way to express agreement.
免 (miǎn) means 'to exempt' and 费 (fèi) means 'fee' or 'cost'. So, 'exempt from fees'.
A pattern used to connect two adjectives or verbs that coexist, often describing qualities of the same subject.

segments.visual_notes: Show a checkmark for '没错', then icons for 'free', 'fast' (a running clock), and 'convenient' (a thumbs-up).

segments.segment_title: Line 17: It doesn't feel as good
segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express that one thing doesn't feel as good as another.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, Xuemei offers a different perspective. She begins with [zh:可是有的时候], 'But sometimes...'. The core of her argument is [zh:看微信、看电邮没有打电话感觉好]. This is a comparison. It means 'reading WeChat and emails doesn't feel as good as making a phone call.' The structure is 'A 没有 B + Adjective'. Here, 'A' is reading messages, 'B' is making a call, and the adjective is implied in [zh:感觉好], 'feels good'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 可是有的时候,看微信、看电邮没有打电话感觉好。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Kěshì yǒu de shíhou, kàn wēixìn, kàn diànyóu méiyǒu dǎ diànhuà gǎnjué hǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: But sometimes, reading WeChat messages and emails doesn't feel as good as making a phone call.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common phrase for 'sometimes', synonymous with 有时候 (yǒushíhou).
Can be used as a noun for 'feeling' or a verb for 'to feel'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a character looking at a phone with a neutral face, then show the same character on a phone call with a happy face.

segments.teaching_goal: To recognize when a new or playful term is being introduced.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming teases Xuemei with a new word. He says, [zh:雪梅喜欢“电聊”]. This means 'Xuemei likes to 'tele-chat'.' The word [zh:电聊] is in quotes, which signals to us that it's a special term, probably a joke or a newly coined word. The friends are about to explain what it means.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 雪梅喜欢“电聊”。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xuěméi xǐhuān 'diànliáo'.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Xuemei likes to 'tele-chat'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A playful portmanteau combining 电 from 电话 (diànhuà, telephone) and 聊 from 聊天 (liáotiān, to chat).

segments.visual_notes: Animate the word '电聊' appearing on screen with quotation marks and a question mark next to it.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the common question pattern '什么是...?' (What is...?).

narration.subtitle_en: As expected, Xuemei is confused and asks for a definition. She repeats the word, [zh:电聊?], and then asks, [zh:什么是“电聊”?]. This is a very common and useful question pattern. [zh:什么是] means 'What is...'. You can use it to ask for the definition of any noun. For example, '什么是网络?' means 'What is the internet?'

on_screen_text.focus_text: “电聊”?什么是“电聊”?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: 'Diànliáo'? Shénme shì 'diànliáo'?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: 'Tele-chat'? What is 'tele-chat'?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A standard phrase used to ask for a definition or explanation of something.

segments.visual_notes: Show a character with a large question mark above her head.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how portmanteau words are formed and explained.

narration.subtitle_en: The friend explains the made-up word. He says, [zh:就是打“电”话“聊”天儿]. This shows how the word was created. It takes [zh:电] from [zh:电话], which means 'telephone,' and [zh:聊] from [zh:聊天儿], which means 'to chat.' He then asks, [zh:懂了吧?], a casual way of saying 'Get it?' or 'Understand now?'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 就是打“电”话“聊”天儿。懂了吧?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Jiùshì dǎ 'diàn'huà 'liáo'tiānr. Dǒng le ba?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: It's 'tele'phone 'chat'ting. Get it?

highlight_words.explanation_en: 电 (diàn) means 'electric' and 话 (huà) means 'speech' or 'talk'.
A colloquial term for chatting. 聊 (liáo) means 'to chat' and 天 (tiān) here means 'sky', so it's like 'chatting about the sky' - casual talk.
A common verb for 'to understand' or 'to know'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the characters '电' from '电话' and '聊' from '聊天儿' flying out and combining to form '电聊'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the pun between '电聊' and '电疗'.

narration.subtitle_en: There's a deeper joke here that relies on sound. The made-up word for 'tele-chat', [zh:电聊], is pronounced exactly the same as another real word: [zh:电疗], which means 'electrotherapy'. So, by saying Xuemei likes to [zh:电聊], her friends are humorously comparing her long phone calls to a medical treatment. This kind of wordplay is very common and fun in Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 电聊 (diànliáo) vs. 电疗 (diànliáo)
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: A pun based on identical pronunciation.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A playful word for chatting on the phone, created from 电话 (telephone) and 聊天 (chat).
A medical term. The humor comes from it sounding identical to 电聊.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of a person on the phone, with small, harmless electricity bolts coming from the phone, making them laugh.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn different adverbs to describe an action as continuous or lasting a long time.

narration.subtitle_en: To exaggerate how much Xuemei chats, the friend uses a series of descriptive phrases. First, [zh:一直聊], meaning 'chatting continuously.' [zh:一直] means 'always' or 'straight on.' Next, [zh:不停地聊], 'chatting non-stop.' [zh:不停] means 'without stopping.' Finally, [zh:从早到晚地聊], 'chatting from morning till night.' This repetition with different adverbs creates a very vivid and funny picture of her phone habits.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 一直聊、不停地聊、从早到晚地聊……
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yìzhí liáo, bù tíng de liáo, cóng zǎo dào wǎn de liáo...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Chatting continuously, non-stop, from morning till night...

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb indicating an action continues without interruption.
Literally 'not stopping'. Used adverbially with 地 (de) to modify a verb.
A phrase indicating a long duration, covering the entire day.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a character chatting on the phone while a sun rises and sets quickly in the background.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express a feeling of being misunderstood and to learn the phrase 'it sounds like'.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker begins with [zh:怎么], which in this context means 'What?' or 'How come?', expressing surprise and a bit of confusion. Next is the phrase [zh:听起来], which means 'it sounds like'. This is a very useful pattern for sharing your impression of something. It's followed by [zh:好像], meaning 'it seems' or 'as if'. The full thought is: 'It sounds as if you don't like chatting with me'. Notice the word [zh:聊天儿], with the 'er' sound at the end, which is a common colloquial touch in northern Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 怎么?听起来好像你不爱跟我聊天儿。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zěnme? Tīng qǐlái hǎoxiàng nǐ bú ài gēn wǒ liáotiānr.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What? It sounds like you don't like chatting with me.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An interrogative pronoun used here to express surprise or confusion, similar to 'What?' or 'How is it that...?'
A resultative complement used to express an impression gained through listening.
An adverb used to indicate that something seems to be the case, expressing an inference or assumption.
A colloquial term for chatting. The '儿' (er) suffix is common in northern Mandarin and often makes words sound more casual.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a question mark popping up next to the character's head. Highlight '听起来' and '好像' as the character processes the information.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to quickly deny something and clarify your intention with 'That's not what I mean'.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker quickly replies with three [zh:不]s. Repeating 'no' like this adds strong emphasis and urgency. Then comes a very important phrase: [zh:我不是那个意思]. This literally means 'I am not that meaning,' and is the perfect way to say 'That's not what I mean.' Finally, they explain their true intention with [zh:我是开玩笑], which means 'I was joking.' This is a great sequence to remember for smoothing over social situations.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 不,不,不,我不是那个意思,我是开玩笑。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bù, bù, bù, wǒ bú shì nàge yìsi, wǒ shì kāi wánxiào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: No, no, no, that's not what I mean. I was joking.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to the meaning or intention behind someone's words or actions.
A verb-object phrase that means to make a joke. '开' means 'to make' and '玩笑' is 'joke'.

segments.visual_notes: Show the word '不' appearing three times rapidly. Then, highlight the full phrase '我不是那个意思' as a single block.

segments.teaching_goal: To master the essential phrase '我不是那个意思' for dispelling misunderstandings.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's focus on the phrase [zh:我不是那个意思]. As we saw in the dialogue, this is your go-to expression when someone has misinterpreted your words or actions. It's polite, direct, and immediately signals that there's been a misunderstanding. While you could technically say [zh:我的意思不是那个], it sounds much less natural. The fixed phrase [zh:我不是那个意思] is what native speakers use almost every time. It's a crucial tool for smooth communication.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我不是那个意思
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: wǒ bú shì nàge yìsi
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: That's not what I mean.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common, fixed phrase used to dispel misunderstandings.

segments.visual_notes: Display the 'Correct' and 'Incorrect' sentences side-by-side, with a large checkmark next to the correct one and a cross next to the incorrect one.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn phrases for reassurance and for changing the topic of conversation.

narration.subtitle_en: After clarifying, the speaker offers reassurance with [zh:我当然爱跟你聊天儿], 'Of course I love chatting with you.' The word [zh:当然] means 'of course'. Then, to move on, they say [zh:好了，好了], which works like 'Alright, alright'. This is followed by [zh:不说这些了], 'let's not talk about this anymore.' Finally, they change the subject by asking, [zh:我们到底去哪家卡拉OK啊]. The word [zh:到底] here means 'after all' or 'in the end,' adding emphasis to the question.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我当然爱跟你聊天儿。好了,好了,不说这些了,我们到底去哪家卡拉OK啊?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ dāngrán ài gēn nǐ liáotiānr. Hǎo le, hǎo le, bù shuō zhèxiē le, wǒmen dàodǐ qù nǎ jiā kǎlā'ōukēi a?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Of course I love chatting with you. Alright, alright, let's not talk about this anymore. Which karaoke place are we going to after all?

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb used to express certainty or agreement, similar to 'certainly' or 'naturally'.
A phrase used to stop a discussion, signal a transition, or indicate that something is settled.
An adverb used in questions to press for a definitive answer, emphasizing curiosity or impatience.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight '当然' for reassurance. Then show '好了,好了' as a way to 'wipe the slate clean'. Finally, emphasize '到底' with an animated, growing question mark.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to describe actions taken online and show consideration for friends.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, the speaker explains their thoughtful planning. They start with [zh:我上网查了], 'I looked it up online.' [zh:上网] means 'to go online' and [zh:查] means 'to check' or 'to look up'. They found a place: [zh:东边儿那家不错], 'That place on the east side is pretty good.' The word [zh:不错] is a very common way to say 'not bad' or 'good'. The reason is [zh:那儿的歌都是丽莎喜欢的], 'they have all the songs Lisha likes.' The final sentence, [zh:你们看，我没忘了朋友吧], means 'See, I didn't forget my friends, did I?' The particle [zh:吧] at the end turns the statement into a tag question, seeking confirmation and showing a bit of pride in being a good friend.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我上网查了,东边儿那家不错,那儿的歌都是丽莎喜欢的。你们看,我没忘了朋友吧?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ shàngwǎng chá le, dōngbianr nà jiā búcuò, nàr de gē dōu shì Lìshā xǐhuān de. Nǐmen kàn, wǒ méi wàng le péngyǒu ba?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I looked it up online. That place on the east side is pretty good, and they have all the songs Lisha likes. See, I didn't forget my friends, did I?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object phrase: '上' means 'to go on' and '网' is 'net' (short for internet).
A verb used for looking up information in a dictionary, online, or from a source.
Literally 'not wrong,' this is a very common adjective for saying something is good or of good quality.
The verb '忘' (to forget) with the particle '了' indicating completion of the action (or in a negative '没忘了', the non-occurrence of forgetting).

segments.visual_notes: Animate a computer screen with a search bar for '上网查了'. Show a map with a pin on the 'east side'. End with the character smiling proudly.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach students how to use potential complements to express whether an action's result is achievable.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's dive into our first grammar point: potential complements. These tell us if an action can be successfully completed. The main structure is 'Verb + [zh:不] + Complement'. In the dialogue, we heard [zh:离不开电脑], which means 'can't tear oneself away from the computer'. The complement [zh:开] indicates separation, and [zh:不] negates the possibility. This implies a subjective inability to leave. Contrast this with [zh:不能离开], which suggests an objective reason, like a rule. Another form is 'Verb + [zh:得了] or [zh:不了]'. This is a more conversational way to say 'can' or 'cannot' do something. For example, [zh:我去不了了] means 'I can't go anymore', which is very similar to [zh:我不能去了].

on_screen_text.focus_text: Verb + 得/不 + Resultative/Directional Complement
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Indicates whether a result is achievable.
on_screen_text.notes: This structure often appears in the negative form.

grammar_points.pattern: 我一会儿都离不开电脑。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒ yīhuìr dōu lí bu kāi diànnǎo.
grammar_points.explanation_en: I can't tear myself away from my computer, not even for a moment. (Subjective inability)

grammar_points.pattern: 我的公寓很小，住不下两个人。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒ de gōngyù hěn xiǎo, zhù bu xià liǎng ge rén.
grammar_points.explanation_en: My apartment is too small for two people. (External condition)

grammar_points.pattern: 那个演唱会我去不了了。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Nàge yǎnchànghuì wǒ qù bu liǎo le.
grammar_points.explanation_en: I won't be able to go to the concert. (Conversational form of 'cannot')

segments.visual_notes: Animate the structure 'V + 不 + C' with '离不开' as the main example. Show an icon of a person stuck to a computer. Then, show the 'V + 不了' structure with an icon of a concert ticket being crossed out.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach students how to use time words and place expressions to create cohesive narratives.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, let's look at how to connect sentences to tell a story smoothly. In Chinese narratives, we often place time words, place expressions, or pronouns at the beginning of a clause to link it to the previous one. Look at the first long sentence of our dialogue. It starts with [zh:昨天], setting the initial time. Then, after describing the situation, the next key event is introduced with [zh:十分钟以后]. Placing these time words at the front makes the sequence of events clear and connected. If you put them after the subject, like '[zh:他们十分钟以后]', the sentence sounds choppy. Always remember: linking words go first to ensure a smooth flow.

on_screen_text.focus_text: Time/Place Word + Subject + Verb...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Use linking words at the beginning of a clause for cohesion.
on_screen_text.notes: This creates a smooth, flowing narrative.

grammar_points.pattern: 昨天...约好...，时间到了...，十分钟以后，才看见...
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Zuótiān... yuē hǎo..., shíjiān dào le..., shí fēnzhōng yǐhòu, cái kànjiàn...
grammar_points.explanation_en: Yesterday... made plans..., when the time came..., ten minutes later, (they) saw...

grammar_points.pattern: 开学前我坐飞机来学校，在飞机上我看了一个电影。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Kāixué qián wǒ zuò fēijī lái xuéxiào, zài fēijī shàng wǒ kàn le yí ge diànyǐng.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Before school started, I flew to school. On the plane, I watched a film.

segments.visual_notes: Show the long sentence from the dialogue. Highlight '昨天' and '十分钟以后' as they are mentioned. Use arrows to show how these words link different parts of the story together.

segments.segment_title: Directional Complements: 过来 & 过去
segments.teaching_goal: To teach the use of directional complements 'guolai' and 'guoqu' to indicate movement relative to the speaker.

narration.subtitle_en: Our final grammar point is about direction. The words [zh:过来] and [zh:过去] are directional complements that tell us about movement relative to the speaker. [zh:过来] means 'to come over' or indicates movement towards the speaker's location or point of view. In the text, his friends saw Tianming [zh:跑过来], 'running over' towards them. Conversely, [zh:过去] means 'to go over', indicating movement away from the speaker. For example, [zh:小王从我身边走过去] means 'Little Wang walked past me,' moving away from my position. So, remember: [zh:过来] is for coming, and [zh:过去] is for going.

on_screen_text.focus_text: Verb + 过来 / 过去
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Verb + guòlai / guòqù
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Indicates direction of movement relative to the speaker.
on_screen_text.notes: 过来 (guòlai) = towards speaker; 过去 (guòqù) = away from speaker.

grammar_points.pattern: 天明急急忙忙地跑过来。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Tiānmíng jíjímángmáng de pǎo guòlai.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Tianming came rushing over. (Movement towards the speaker/observer)

grammar_points.pattern: 小王从我身边走过去。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Xiǎo Wáng cóng wǒ shēnbiān zǒu guòqù.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Little Wang walked past me. (Movement away from the speaker)

segments.visual_notes: Use simple animations. For '过来', show a character running towards the screen. For '过去', show a character walking away from the screen or past the camera's viewpoint.

segments.teaching_goal: To review the key vocabulary and grammar patterns from the lesson.

narration.subtitle_en: Great job! Let's quickly review the key phrases from our lesson on computers and the internet. These expressions will help you talk about modern life and technology in Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: Key Phrases Review

highlight_words.translation: Can't be away from the computer
As a result, forgot the time

segments.visual_notes: Display the key phrases as a list on a final summary card. Each phrase can appear one by one.
